column1: Screened passenger traffic at the largest airports in Canada 1 2

column2: Halifax/Robert L Stanfield International, Nova Scotia
not available for a specific reference period
Screened traffic includes both passengers and non-passengers screened at Pre-Board Screening (PBS) checkpoints at Canada's eight largest airports.

There could be instances where data are incomplete due to technical issues related to data capture from the Canadian Air Transport Security Authority’s (CATSA) Boarding Pass Security System. Technical issues are typically short in duration and have minimal impact on monthly traffic volumes. However, when a technical issue results in a notable impact on the monthly statistics, information about the issue and the impact can be found here (opens new window)."

Non-passenger data are comprised of non-passengers screened at Pre-Board Screening (PBS) checkpoints but does not include non-passengers screened at Non-Passenger Screening (NPS) checkpoints. Some examples of non-passengers screened at PBS checkpoints include flight and cabin crews as well as airport staff who aid passengers through the PBS checkpoints.
